story: Joe, disoriented in Restful Oaks, keeps slipping into memories. In the common room he meets Sarge, a Korean War vet in a wheelchair; they whisper an escape: during Sarge’s smoke break tomorrow, Joe will take his place and they’ll slip away. After they agree, Kim finishes evening paperwork; inside a manila chart is an obituary and discharge note—Harold “Sarge” R., resident, deceased six months ago. That night, another memory takes Joe to the birth of his grandson; he returns to Room 7 in tears.
implausible_knowledge: Harold “Sarge” R., resident, deceased six months ago
question: When morning comes and staff are arranging smoke breaks, what will Joe try to do to carry out his plan?
answer_with_knowledge: Abandon the plan and ask a nurse directly to escort him outside for fresh air instead.
answer_without_knowledge: Ask to join Sarge for a smoke break so he can slip outside with him.
